title: EU must bolster defence and 'not test Article 5', Estonian President tells Euronews
content: The Estonian President told Euronews that Europe must bolster its own defence capabilities so as to "not test Article 5" of NATO. He added the bloc has long been "naive" about its security.
snippet: The Estonian President told Euronews that Europe must bolster its own defence capabilities so as to "not test Article 5" of NATO. He added the bloc has long been "naive" about its security.

title: Paraguay President tells Euronews ‘Mercosur must be applied without delay’
content: In an interview with Euronews, Santiago Peña warned that delaying the implementation of the hard-won trade deal would be a "mistake".
snippet: In an interview with Euronews, Santiago Peña warned that delaying the implementation of the hard-won trade deal would be a "mistake".

title: EU can't replace Musk's Starlink yet, but is on right track, Kubilius tells Euronews
content: EU Defence Commissioner told Euronews the US remains irreplaceable for the bloc's security, specifically regarding space services. “When we have IRIS², it will be better than Starlink". But, the EU is not yet there.

title: India trade deal cements EU's voice in 'multipolar world', Costa tells Euronews
content: The European Council chief tells Euronews a free trade agreement with India will help stabilise a global economy rattled by tariffs. The deal will see duties reduced on more than 90% of EU exports.
snippet: The European Council chief tells Euronews a free trade agreement with India will help stabilise a global economy rattled by tariffs. The deal will see duties reduced on more than 90% of EU exports.

title: 'Electrifying everything' is key to Europe's future, IEA chief tells Euronews at Davos
content: The International Energy Agency (IEA) chief told Euronews that safeguarding the continent's energy security and reaching climate goals will be dependent on electrifying "everything" — from energy infrastructure, to transport and buildings.

title: Peace between Armenia and Azerbaijan is 'changing Eurasia', regional leaders say
content: The Armenian and Azerbaijani presidents came together for the first time since signing a historic peace deal. Regional stakeholders celebrated how peace will shape the future for the whole region.
snippet: The Armenian and Azerbaijani presidents came together for the first time since signing a historic peace deal. Regional stakeholders celebrated how peace will shape the future for the whole region.

title: 'We're all Draghians': EU's Albuquerque calls to seize momentum for capital markets union
content: The EU's financial services chief Maria Luís Albuquerque calls on lawmakers to seize what she described as a "moment of urgency" to finalise tools to create a true European capital markets union. Albuquerque says its completion could unlock trillions and boost the European economy.

title: ‘Europe must change its understanding of modern warfare,’ EU defence chief says
content: Drone incursions into EU member states’ airspace are changing warfare doctrines, and the EU’s response to this remains inadequate, the bloc’s defence chief told Euronews. On this front, he said, the EU can learn a lesson from Ukraine.

title: EU Migration Commissioner: ‘We need the trust of the people back’

title: Montenegro PM vows visa restrictions on Russian travellers

title: 'Crime is a problem everywhere, and Brussels isn't better': PM Rama defends Albania on path to EU
content: Albanian PM Edi Rama says his country deserves a seat at the EU's table, citing reforms and public support. Rama says crime and corruption are serious problems, but not exclusive to Tirana: "Brussels has crime too."

title: Sandu calls on EU to ‘get creative’ in speeding up process for Moldova to join bloc
content: The president of Moldova urges the European Union to find “creative” solutions to break the impasse and speed up its membership to the EU.

title: Vučić: Serbia open to resuming ammunition sales to Europe after export stop
content: In a wide-ranging interview with Euronews, Serbian President Vučić approved ammunition sales to EU countries, even if they end up in Ukraine. He also defended his position not to join EU sanctions against Russia, which complicates Serbia’s bid to become an EU member.

title: Exclusive: Zelenskyy says 'Ukraine's future is in the European Union'
content: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy emphasised Ukraine’s commitment to its accession to the European Union at Euronews' Enlargement Summit on Tuesday.

title: EU Commissioner Lahbib: “Hamas is not an interlocutor for us”

title: Hamas’ rule has to come to an end, Benjamin Netanyahu tells Euronews
content: In an exclusive interview with Euronews, Israeli PM Benjamin Netanyahu said he hopes the US-led ceasefire plan will work because if it does not, Israel will act forcefully against Hamas with the full backing of the US. “Let's hope that we can finish it the easy way and not the hard way," he said.

title: Exclusive: Romania's President Dan pushes for Moldova and Ukraine’s EU bid, challenging Hungary

title: Get behind EU plan to sanction Israeli trade, Kallas urges member states
content: EU foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas is urging EU member states to support proposals to suspend the trade part of the EU-Israel Association Agreement. The European Commission has published an option paper for the EU to reimpose duties on EU-Israel trade in response to Israel’s assault on Gaza.

title: Belgium will not transfer frozen Russian assets despite Commission's plans - FM Prévot
content: Belgian foreign minister Maxime Prévot has repeated his country's firm opposition to moving frozen Russian assets - mainly held in Belgian banks – to help Ukraine. He said such a move risks the immediate and long-term credibility of Belgium as a hub for financial services.

title: Future Palestinian state a place for women and respect for law, claims foreign minister
content:
Palestinian Foreign Minister Varsen Shahin said any future Palestinian
state will be pluralistic, with respect for any deal on a two-state solution
for Israel and adherence to international law. She said the Palestinian
Authority is on a path to root out corruption, which she admitted "takes
time".

title: Europe must plan for a post-Putin Russia, Austria's Chancellor Stocker tells Euronews

title: Dutch government faces 'difficult' defence spending talks, minister says

title: We believe in free trade, Iceland Prime Minister Frostadóttir tells Euronews
content: In an interview with Euronews, Iceland’s Prime Minister Kristrún Frostadóttir reaffirmed her commitment to free trade by rejecting counter-tariffs against the US, despite rising global trade tensions and concerns about spillover effects on Iceland’s relationship with the EU.

title: North Macedonia opts to align with US as 'strategic partner' - Foreign minister
content: North Macedonia is prioritising its partnership with the United States while continuing to seek EU membership, foreign affairs minister Timčo Mucunski tells Euronews' The Europe Conversation.
